<!-- generated by sofar — do not hand-edit; truth lives in events.jsonl -->

# Decisions: speed

- 2026-07-20T08:43:08.062Z — chose **T1 gate-relevant drift = Option B, mutation-class only: the existing staleness/nudge counter total (freshnessTotal over freshness.events_since_writeback — file_touched + command_run + task_status_changed + note_added + decision_logged), initiative-scoped, read-side, computed at Stop; zero drift exits 0 silently even without a session_ended, nonzero or ANY drift-computation error enforces exactly today's block (fail closed). Gate only ever converts an exit-2 into an exit-0 — every today-exit-0 path (loop guard first, unreadable stdin, unbound repo, unregistered session, written-back session) is untouched.** over Option A, any event since last write-back — rejected because the SessionStart hook's own session_started event makes drift nonzero for every session, so the silent exit would never fire (dead code); also considered widening B to count plan_updated/phase_status_changed, rejected for this task. because B reuses the ONE existing drift definition shared by the UserPromptSubmit nudge and the staleness line, so gate/nudge/staleness can never disagree, and pure reads emit no events so they naturally never gate. Accepted gaps, stated: (1) plan_updated/phase_status_changed stay uncounted, matching the staleness counter today — a plan-only session ends ungated since the plan event is already durable in the record; widening the counter later must widen all three surfaces together via a new decision. (2) Drift is initiative-scoped, so a read-only session on a record left drifted by an earlier writer still gates — it becomes the write-back of record for that drift; no regression, that session is blocked today too. Existing zero-drift Stop tests (hooks.test.ts) are amended to seed drift; SPEC §Hooks + §Acceptance Phase 3 wording updated to drift-qualified enforcement.
- 2026-07-20T08:45:54.478Z — chose **T1 refinement: the Stop gate's silent exit requires BOTH zero initiative-wide freshness drift AND no derived mechanical activity (BD44 session.activity) on the stopping session itself — gate-relevant drift = counter total OR own-session activity.** over The pure initiative-scoped counter alone (the first T1 decision as originally scoped), which the Phase 7 acceptance disproved: concurrent session A's write-back resets the shared counter, so unwritten session B — with real task/file work — would exit silently and its summary/next_action would never land, breaking the Phase 7 "independent Stop gates" law. because session.activity is an existing read-side derivation (zero new event types) that attributes file_touched/command_run/task_status_changed by envelope.session, so an unwritten session that did mechanical work stays gated regardless of who reset the shared counter. Known asymmetry accepted and documented: activity excludes note_added/decision_logged (BD44 scope), so a session whose ONLY act was a decision/note, absorbed by a later concurrent write-back, ends ungated — the event is durable in the record and a postdating write-back acknowledged it. All five commissioned T1 behaviors unchanged; phase-7 tests pass unmodified.
- 2026-07-20T08:54:32.388Z — chose **T3: serve the frozen 7-tool MCP surface over streamable HTTP at /mcp on the EXISTING `sofar serve` daemon (SDK StreamableHTTPServerTransport, sessions keyed by mcp-session-id), with a FRESH SofarServerHandle — own ToolContext and active-session pin — created per MCP session, and all transports closed on daemon close. stdio (`sofar mcp`) stays the default and the only transport `sofar init` registers; HTTP is opt-in via a documented .mcp.json entry {type:"http", url:"http://127.0.0.1:4173/mcp"}. Zero new dependencies (SDK 1.29.0 already ships both transports).** over (a) One shared Server/ToolContext for all HTTP clients — rejected: the BD58 active-session pin would be shared across concurrent Claude sessions, recreating exactly the Phase 11 misroute that Phase 12 hardened against. (b) A separate `sofar mcp --http` daemon — rejected: a second long-lived process to manage defeats the no-per-session-spawn goal; serve is already the resident localhost process. (c) Making HTTP the default — rejected: zero-config users must lose nothing. because Sessions connect to a running server instead of paying a per-session `sofar mcp` spawn. Guard-rail reading: "the state server serves JSON on localhost — nothing more" holds — /mcp is JSON-RPC bound to 127.0.0.1, no UI/sync/telemetry, and the 7-tool surface is byte-frozen (transport change only, parity-tested stdio vs HTTP). Daemon-absent fallback is fail-fast connection refusal (never a hang, pinned by test); documented remedy: start `sofar serve` or keep the stdio config. Serve banner wording gains "/mcp MCP" — the cli-ui banner pins update with it.
